theory.intro: Rounding a decimal keeps it to a stated number of decimal places by looking at the next digit.
facts: Look at the digit **just after** the place you are rounding to.
If it is $5$ or more, round **up**; if it is $4$ or less, round **down**.
Drop all digits after the rounding place.
steps: Find the place you are rounding to (1 dp = tenths, 2 dp = hundredths).
Look at the next digit to its right.
Round up or keep the digit, then drop the rest.
